messages.content: The kind of malware that holds a computer system or the data captive until a payment is made is called?
The kind of malware that holds a computer system or data captive until a payment is made is called ransomware.
What prevents undesirable traffic from entering protected areas of the network?
Firewalls typically prevent undesirable traffic from entering protected areas of a network.

Which of the following is an appropriate combination for a two-factor authentication system?



Select one:

a.
ATM card + voice recognition


b.
Smartphone + credit card


c.
Credit card + ATM card


d.
Facial recognition + voice recognition
d. Facial recognition + voice recognition
The encrypted form of a message is called ?
The encrypted form of a message is called ciphertext.

Which of the following is/are a type of malware?

i) Spyware

ii) Bot

iii) Firmware


Select one:

a.
i) and ii) only


b.
iii) only


c.
i) only


d.
i), ii) and iii)
d. i), ii) and iii)
Transforming an encrypted message back into its original form is called?
Transforming an encrypted message back into its original form is called decryption.

“Ensuring that assets can be modified by authorized parties only” is related to which of the following components of security?

Select one:

a.
Confidentiality

b.
Availability

c.
Integrity

d.
None of the answers
c. Integrity

Which of the following is/are considerations in physical security?

i) Using surveillance cameras to monitor the entrance.

ii) Setting up a disaster recovery policy for a data centre.

iii) Installing firewall for a network.

Select one:

a.
ii) and iii) only


b.
i), ii) and iii)


c.
i) and ii) only

d.
i) only
c. i) and ii) only
What integrity refers to the source of the data?

Integrity, in terms of data, refers to the trustworthiness and reliability of the source from which the data originates. It ensures that the data has not been altered, tampered with, or modified by unauthorized parties, and that it retains its accuracy and completeness.
Which integrity refers to the source of the data?

The term you may be referring to is "Data Integrity." Data integrity ensures that data remains accurate, consistent, and reliable throughout its lifecycle, protecting it from unauthorized modifications, corruptions, or unauthorized access.

If a person uses his/her name as the password, this creates a/n ____________ in the system.

Select one:

a.
threat


b.
vulnerability


c.
control


d.
attack
b. vulnerability
